# nomad_job (Resource)



## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "nomad_job" "app" {
  jobspec = file("${path.module}/jobspec.hcl")
}

resource "nomad_job" "app" {
  jobspec = <<EOT
job "foo" {
  datacenters = ["dc1"]
  type        = "service"
  group "foo" {
    task "foo" {
      driver = "raw_exec"
      config {
        command = "/bin/sleep"
        args    = ["1"]
      }

      resources {
        cpu    = 20
        memory = 10
      }

      logs {
        max_files     = 3
        max_file_size = 10
      }
    }
  }
}
EOT
}

resource "nomad_job" "app" {
  jobspec = file("${path.module}/jobspec.json")
  json    = true
}

resource "nomad_job" "app" {
  hcl2 {
    vars = {
      "restart_attempts" = "5",
      "datacenters"      = "[\"dc1\", \"dc2\"]",
    }
  }

  jobspec = <<EOT
variable "datacenters" {
  type = list(string)
}

variable "restart_attempts" {
  type = number
}

job "foo-hcl2" {
  datacenters = var.datacenters

  restart {
    attempts = var.restart_attempts
    ...
  }
  ...
}
EOT
}

resource "random_pet" "random_dc" {}

# This resource will fail to plan because random_pet.random_dc.id is unknown.
resource "nomad_job" "job_with_hcl2" {
  jobspec = <<EOT
variable "datacenter" {
  type = string
}

job "example" {
  datacenters = [var.datacenter]
  ...
}
EOT

  hcl2 {
    vars = {
      datacenter = random_pet.random_dc.id
    }
  }
}

# This will work since Terraform will provide a fully rendered jobspec once it
# knows the value of random_pet.random_dc.id.
resource "nomad_job" "job_with_hcl2" {
  jobspec = <<EOT
job "example" {
  datacenters = ["${random_pet.random_dc.id}"]
  ...
}
EOT
}

resource "nomad_job" "app" {
  jobspec = file("${path.module}/jobspec.hcl")

  hcl2 {
    allow_fs = true
  }
}

# main.tf

data "local_file" "index_html" {
  filename = "${path.module}/index.html"
}

resource "nomad_job" "nginx" {
  jobspec = templatefile("${path.module}/nginx.nomad.tpl", {
    index_html = data.local_file.index_html.content
  })
}

# nginx.nomad.tpl

job "nginx" {
...
      template {
        data        = <<EOF
${index_html}
EOF
        destination = "local/www/index.html"
      }
...
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `jobspec` (String) Job specification. If you want to point to a file use the file() function.

### Optional

- `deregister_on_destroy` (Boolean) If true, the job will be deregistered on destroy.
- `deregister_on_id_change` (Boolean) If true, the job will be deregistered when the job ID changes.
- `detach` (Boolean) If true, the provider will return immediately after creating or updating, instead of monitoring.
- `hcl2` (Block List, Max: 1) Configuration for the HCL2 jobspec parser. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--hcl2))
- `json` (Boolean) If true, the `jobspec` will be parsed as json instead of HCL.
- `policy_override` (Boolean) Override any soft-mandatory Sentinel policies that fail.
- `preserve_counts` (Boolean) If true, preserve the current task group counts during job registration instead of using the counts from the jobspec.
- `purge_on_destroy` (Boolean) Whether to purge the job when the resource is destroyed.
- `read_allocation_ids` (Boolean, Deprecated)
- `rerun_if_dead` (Boolean) If true, forces the job to run again on apply if it is currently dead
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `all_at_once` (Boolean) Whether the scheduler can make partial placements on oversubscribed nodes.
- `allocation_ids` (List of String, Deprecated) The IDs for allocations associated with this job.
- `constraints` (List of Object) The job constraints. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--constraints))
- `create_index` (Number) The creation index of the job.
- `datacenters` (Set of String) The target datacenters for the job, as derived from the jobspec.
- `deployment_id` (String) If detach = false, the ID for the deployment associated with the last job create/update, if one exists.
- `deployment_status` (String) If detach = false, the status for the deployment associated with the last job create/update, if one exists.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `modify_index` (String) Integer that increments for each change. Used to detect any changes between plan and apply.
- `name` (String) The name of the job, as derived from the jobspec.
- `namespace` (String) The namespace of the job, as derived from the jobspec.
- `parent_id` (String) The parent job ID, if applicable.
- `periodic_config` (List of Object) The job's periodic configuration for time-based scheduling. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--periodic_config))
- `priority` (Number) The priority of the job for scheduling and resource access.
- `region` (String) The target region for the job, as derived from the jobspec.
- `stable` (Boolean) Whether the job is stable.
- `status` (String) The status of the job.
- `status_description` (String) The status description of the job.
- `stop` (Boolean) Whether the job is stopped.
- `submit_time` (String) The time the job was submitted.
- `task_groups` (List of Object) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--task_groups))
- `type` (String) The type of the job, as derived from the jobspec.
- `update_strategy` (List of Object) The update strategy for rolling updates and canary deployments. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--update_strategy))
- `version` (Number) The version of the job.

<a id="nestedblock--hcl2"></a>
### Nested Schema for `hcl2`

Optional:

- `allow_fs` (Boolean) If true, HCL2 file system functions will be enabled when parsing the `jobspec`.
- `vars` (Map of String) Additional variables to use when templating the job with HCL2


<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String)
- `update` (String)

Read-Only:

- `ltarget` (String)
- `operand` (String)
- `rtarget` (String)

Read-Only:

- `prohibit_overlap` (Boolean)
- `spec` (String)
- `spec_type` (String)
- `timezone` (String)

Read-Only:

- `task` (List of Object) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedobjatt--task_groups--task))
- `update_strategy` (List of Object) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedobjatt--task_groups--update_strategy))
- `volumes` (List of Object) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedobjatt--task_groups--volumes))

- `volume_mounts` (List of Object) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedobjatt--task_groups--task--volume_mounts))
